skill.name: zoom-out
skill.description: Ask the agent to step back from a specific code area and explain the wider module, caller, and domain context.
skill.summary: Get a higher-level map of unfamiliar code and how it fits into the project.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 3 as a weak cryptographic algorithm, but that line is only a natural-language description. No cryptographic code, command execution, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection text was found in the reviewed skill content.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 3 contains a plain description asking the agent for broader code context. It does not reference or implement any cryptographic algorithm, so the reported high-risk pattern is dismissed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains only natural-language skill metadata and no code. I did not find evidence of a weak cryptographic algorithm or related security behavior.

content.value_statement: Developers often get stuck inside a single file without seeing the surrounding system. This skill asks Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to map related modules, callers, and domain concepts.
actual_capabilities: Requests a higher-level explanation of unfamiliar code areas.
Asks for relevant modules and callers around the current code.
Encourages use of the project domain glossary and vocabulary.
Helps reframe local implementation details within system architecture.
Supports codebase onboarding, debugging, and refactoring discussions.
limitations: It does not inspect files by itself without an agent that can read the project.
It does not generate diagrams unless the active agent chooses to create them.
It does not enforce a fixed output format or checklist.
It depends on available project context and repository access.

use_cases.title: Understand a New Code Area
use_cases.description: Ask for a map of modules, callers, and domain terms before changing unfamiliar code.

use_cases.title: Plan a Refactor
use_cases.description: Identify surrounding responsibilities and dependencies before moving or simplifying code.

use_cases.title: Review a Complex Change
use_cases.description: Step back from implementation details and explain how the change affects the wider system.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Context Map
prompt_templates.scenario: You are looking at an unfamiliar file.
prompt_templates.prompt: I do not know this area well. Zoom out and explain the surrounding modules, callers, and domain terms.

prompt_templates.title: Caller and Dependency View
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to understand how a component is reached.
prompt_templates.prompt: Zoom out from this component. Show the main callers, dependencies, and data flow in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Planning View
prompt_templates.scenario: You are considering a structural change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before suggesting edits, zoom out. Explain the responsibilities around this code and where a refactor would have impact.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Vocabulary Review
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a domain-focused explanation for a complex area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Zoom out and describe this area using the project domain vocabulary. Include relevant modules, entry points, and ownership boundaries.

output_examples.input: Zoom out from the billing service before I change it.
output_examples.output: The agent describes the billing entry points, payment provider boundary, invoice workflow, and related callers.

output_examples.input: I found this validator but do not know who uses it.
output_examples.output: The agent lists likely callers, explains the validation role, and connects it to the surrounding domain process.

output_examples.input: Help me review this pull request at a higher level.
output_examples.output: The agent summarizes the affected modules, explains system impact, and notes areas that need closer review.

best_practices: Use it before editing code that crosses module or domain boundaries.
Ask for callers, dependencies, and domain vocabulary in the same request.
Follow up with targeted questions after the high-level map is clear.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when you only need a small syntax fix.
Do not accept the map as complete without checking important files.
Do not use it as a replacement for tests or code ownership review.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It asks the agent to step back and explain the broader code context around an unfamiliar area.

faq.question: Does it modify code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a prompt pattern for understanding context before making changes.

faq.question: Can it find callers and dependencies?
faq.answer: Yes, when the active agent has enough repository context or tool access to inspect the project.

faq.question: Is it useful for onboarding?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps new contributors learn how modules, callers, and domain terms fit together.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The skill content does not request network access or external services.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
